name: 2PM
description:
'''2PM''' ({{Korean|hangul=투피엠}}) is a South Korean [[boy band]] formed by [[JYP Entertainment]] in 2008 composed of six members: [[Jun. K]] (formerly known as Junsu), [[Nichkhun]], [[Ok Taec-yeon|Taecyeon]], [[Jang Wooyoung|Wooyoung]], [[Lee Jun-ho|Junho]], and [[Hwang Chan-sung|Chansung]]. Originally a seven-piece group, [[Jay Park|Jaebeom]] left the band in 2009. Unlike most Korean boy bands at the time, 2PM constructed a tough and masculine image through their intense performances often featuring acrobatic stunts and sex appeal. As a result, 2PM is known for creating the ''jimseung-dol'' (translating to "beast idol" in Korean) phenomenon within South Korea. As the group grew older, they stopped incorporating acrobatics in their choreography routines and transitioned into a more gentlemanly image. The group has achieved commercial success in several regions in Asia, including South Korea, Japan, and Thailand, as a leading figure of the [[Korean Wave]].

Through the reality TV series ''Hot Blood Men'', Korean producer [[Park Jin-young|J.Y. Park]] formed an eleven-member band known as [[One Day (South Korean band)|One Day]]. Eventually, the band was split into the dance-focused group 2PM and the ballad-oriented group [[2AM (band)|2AM]]. 2PM debuted with the song "[[10 Out of 10 (2PM song)|10 Out of 10]]" on September 4, 2008. They achieved their first number-one single with "[[Again & Again (2PM song)|Again & Again]]" in 2009, which contributed to them winning the 2009 [[MAMA Award for Artist of the Year]]. Both singles, along with their hit song "[[Heartbeat (2PM song)|Heartbeat]]", were featured on their debut studio album, ''[[01:59PM]]'' (2009), which topped the first issue of the [[Circle Album Chart|Gaon Album Chart]]. From 2010 to 2011, they cemented their popularity across Asia with their streak of hit songs: "[[Without U (2PM song)|Without U]]", "[[I'll Be Back (2PM song)|I'll Be Back]]", and "[[Hands Up (2PM song)|Hands Up]]", all of which were included in their second studio album, [[Hands Up (album)|''Hands Up'']] (2011). 

2PM ventured into the Japanese music scene in 2011 with their debut Japanese single "[[Take Off (2PM song)|Take Off]]" and their debut Japanese studio album, ''[[Republic of 2PM]].'' As the group began writing and producing more of their own music, they enjoyed significant commercial success in South Korea with their subsequent albums: ''[[Grown (album)|Grown]]'' (2013), ''[[Go Crazy (album)|Go Crazy!]]'' (2014), ''[[No.5 (2PM album)|No.5]]'' (2015), and ''[[Gentlemen's Game]]'' (2016), each of which peaked within the top 3 on the Gaon Album Chart. 2PM achieved even greater success in Japan, with their albums ''[[Legend of 2PM]]'' (2013), ''[[Genesis of 2PM]]'' (2014), ''[[2PM of 2PM]]'' (2015), and ''[[Galaxy of 2PM]]'' (2016) all topping the [[Oricon Albums Chart]]. Furthermore, out of their twelve Japanese singles, nine have been certified Gold by the [[Recording Industry Association of Japan]] (RIAJ) for selling over 100,000 copies; ''Galaxy of 2PM'' also has received this certification. 

The group took an official hiatus in 2017 due to [[Conscription in South Korea|mandatory military service]]. Following the members' discharge, 2PM returned with their seventh Korean studio album ''[[Must (album)|Must]]'' and Japanese EP ''[[With Me Again]]'' in 2021.
